overall_plot: Spheria is a series of novels by Cody Leet, first published in 2016. At its core, the series poses a deceptively simple question: if you created a world populated by sentient beings, gave them their own physics, their own mythology, and their own drive to understand existence, what would they discover? And what might their discoveries reveal about our own?

The series follows two intertwined narratives. On one side are the Polyans: crystalline, polygon-based beings who live on the interior surface of a hollow sphere, governed by laws of physics entirely unlike our own, where blue rock repels, red rock attracts, and the sun is a glowing orb of infinite energy floating at the center of their world. They have castes, legends, rituals, and gods. They hunt, grieve, build, and wonder. They are, in every meaningful sense, a civilization.

On the other side are their creators: a small team of scientists, running a quantum computing simulation called the Spheria Project. Each Polyan is animated by a miniature quantum computing unit called a Qube, which governs something disturbingly close to a soul. The humans watch, intervene occasionally as gods, and grapple with the ethical weight of what they've made, especially as the Polyans begin pushing against the boundaries of their reality and inching toward the truth.

Book One, Eversion, follows the collision of these two worlds. Among the Polyans, a spiritual leader named Sa·ma and his apprentice Le·ma uncover the existence of something beyond their world: a portal, a glimpse behind the curtain, just as their society fractures under the rise of a tyrant named Fa·ro, who engineers his own godhood and enslaves the lower castes. On the human side, the project is quietly being sold out from within: Director Dana Carter is running a shadow operation, diverting project resources to an outside benefactor, and eliminating anyone who gets too close to the truth. The two crises converge as the Polyans mount a desperate resistance and the humans race to stop a betrayal that could destroy everything they've built, and everyone inside it.

Olivia's Log is a prequel novella that fills in a crucial gap. It follows Olivia Holland, a Spheria Project intern, in the weeks before the events of Book One. Curious and principled, Olivia stumbles onto Dana's secret server room using an unlikely tool, a borrowed thermal imager, and begins to piece together the conspiracy. What starts as a puzzle becomes a threat. Dana offers her silence, then eliminates her when silence isn't enough. It's a tight, propulsive story that reframes the main novel's human thread and gives Dana's villainy its full weight.
Book Two, Annulus, is currently in development. It will explore a new region of the world with new rules, new entities, and new challenges.
The series is planned across seven books in total.
themes: Betrayal and corruption of idealistic visions
Military-industrial complex and weaponization of peaceful technology
Sacrifice for the greater good (Le·ma's cannon launch)
The price of freedom and organized resistance
Deception and hidden agendas within trusted relationships
Resourcefulness and innovation in desperate circumstances
Bridge-building (literal and metaphorical) to impossible destinations
Genocide and mass violence in pursuit of power
The corrupting influence of absolute power (Fa·ro)

timeline.text: Olivia meditates on the Academic Building roof; a seagull interrupts her
Randy visits Farmington and uses a thermal imager to spy on Ashley in the shower; Olivia kicks him out
Randy sends a breakup email and asks for his thermal imager back; Olivia keeps the device
Olivia and Ashley drink scorpion bowls at the Butterfly lounge; Ashley signs them up for karaoke
Max asks Olivia to make a sign for the lab open house; she goes to the 6th floor storage room
Olivia witnesses Dana enter a hidden room via keypad; uses thermal imager to read the code 2465
Olivia returns alone at 11pm, enters the secret red Qube server room, and creates a hidden admin account with Wow! Signal passcode 6EQUJ5
Dana catches Olivia in the secret room and bribes her with $400,000 to stay silent, threatening to name her as an accomplice
Olivia encodes the floor and keypad code into her journal using an acrostic cipher and attempts to email it to Ashley as insurance
The email fails to send — network connection lost — unknown to Olivia
Olivia returns to the roof to meditate and resolves to tell Max in the morning
Dana pushes Olivia off the roof; Olivia's last word is 'No'
Max presents research at prestigious 25th Solvay Conference on Physics in Brussels
Jill commits suicide at UConn Health Center library entrance, traumatizing Tim
Min interviews for internship to replace deceased Jill, films interview with spy glasses
Young Sa·ma discovers Lumen Grotto and is tested by old Lumen Master
Le·ma selected as Sa·ma's apprentice and taken on Lumen Walk journey through Uplift Pass
Le·ma and Sa·ma reach the Rift at world's edge; Le·ma drops yellow shard into bottomless chasm
Sa·ma abandons Le·ma at Rift camp for solo return journey as final test
Sa·ma discovers legendary Fertile Field and receives artifact sensor with three triangles from the gods
Sa·ma uses artifact sensor to command attacking Zalisk to spare Le·ma's life
Le·ma faces final test: must find way over vertical blue mountains with no passage on return side
Min begins work at Spheria Project, assigned to reception desk with data analysis duties
Jean observes Polyan Colony construction in VR, watches Drones and Builders making new hive
Dana reviews logs showing Polyan anthropology activity exceeding engineering for first time; unusual behaviors increasing
Graham's airship 'Buruwa Gunya' arrives moored to UConn Health Center roof
Gizmodo publishes article 'Scientists Play God In This Virtual Experiment' bringing public attention to Spheria project
Four consecutive red bubbles rise toward Source, threatening Colony with flooding; Council debates relocation
Sa·ma discovers golden cave with glowing plants and portal to Qubessence, encounters cave god
Fa·ro forces Sa·ma to lead expedition back to golden cave with Ga·zo and Za·zo to reach portal
Sa·ma imprisoned in repurposed Council building - Polyans' first use of incarceration
Min's computer crashes with system failure before critical Graham meeting
Ga·zo convinces Council to create second seven-legged god; preparation ritual begins as violet bubble approaches
Mysterious figure meets at Electric Boat submarine facility during storm, suggesting external threats or espionage
Min discovers secret red server room beneath Qube floor with Tor network and unauthorized military equipment
Fa·ro reveals he eliminated the Council and declares himself sole dictator of the Colony
Min hacks secret room computer using Olivia's password 6EQUJ5 and discovers evidence of weapons project
Max confronts and fights Min in the secret server room, chasing her through damaged equipment
Ga·zo reports all Workers have vanished overnight; Fa·ro locates them gathered near the tower using his artifact
Sa·ma's beam cannon fires and destroys tower support columns, causing massive structure to fall across the Rift creating a bridge
Max traps Min and Dana in hospital incinerator which activates; they escape through chimney with help from volunteer Walter (Ch 45-47)
Le·ma is launched from Sa·ma's cannon, crashes into Fa·ro during battle, and steals his artifact
Le·ma uses stolen artifact to control Zalisk herd which attacks Soldiers while protecting Workers, enabling mass escape across bridge
At board meeting, video confession plays showing Dana admitting to Olivia's murder and DARPA deal; Captain Brennan arrives to arrest her
Dana draws gun on Captain Brennan when confronted about her crimes
Spheria team reviews recordings of tower bridge creation and Worker liberation battle as Max commands playback

human.name: Max
human.description: Thin, dark-haired physicist who presented at the 25th Solvay Conference. Leader of the Spheria Project. Chronically late, somewhat socially awkward, prone to philosophical musings.

human.name: Min
human.description: Vietnamese-American computer science student with 'spy glasses' that record everything. Enthusiastic about technology. Brings Goi Cuon to team gatherings. Becomes reception desk worker/data analyst.

human.name: David
human.role: Chair of 25th Solvay Conference on Physics
human.description: Max's friend from online quantum theory forums. Youthful demeanor despite age. Helped Max get speaking invitation to prestigious physics conference.

human.name: Tim
human.description: Student at UConn Health Center. Witnessed traumatic suicide of previous intern (Olivia). Makes frozen drinks at happy hour. Works on Spheria Project.

human.name: Jill
human.description: Student at UConn Health Center. Was tramatized when Olivia's body landed near her.

human.name: Sheila
human.description: Australian medical student at UConn Health Center. Attractive ('knockout'). Found internship posting for Min. Doesn't understand computers but supportive friend.

human.name: Jean
human.description: Team member who observes Spheria using VR headset and 3D SpaceMouse. Energized by big meals. Not a video game player. Uses ritual entry method to observe Polyans.

human.name: Olivia Holland
human.description: Summer intern working on statistical analysis for the Spheria project. Keeps a work journal documenting her experiences, personal life struggles including a breakup with Randy, and developing friendship with roommate Ashley.

human.name: Moses Diego
human.description: Tech journalist who previously covered the Qube technology and returns to write an article titled 'Scientists Play God In This Virtual Experiment' about the Spheria project.

human.name: Walter
human.description: Hospital volunteer who discovers Min and Max trapped in the incinerator by following mysterious banging sounds

human.name: Captain Brennan
human.description: Security officer who investigated Olivia's death and arrives to arrest Dana; had doubts about the suicide ruling

human.name: Graham
human.description: Wealthy billionaire who funds the Spheria Project with a pacifist vision. Owns the luxury yellow airship 'Buruwa Gunya.' His grant included stipulations such as building the mooring mast on the UConn Health Center roof. Present at the board meeting where Dana's betrayal is exposed.

human.name: Dana Carter
human.description: Manager of the Spheria Project who secretly arranged a deal with DARPA to weaponize the technology in exchange for personal funding and land in Costa Rica. Murdered intern Olivia Holland to cover up the conspiracy. Built and operated a secret duplicate server room beneath the Qube floor.

human.name: Frankie
human.description: Team member on the Spheria Project who startles Min while she is scrutinizing data. Debates with Min about whether the world is mathematical formulas or beautiful random natural patterns. Invites Min to go collect textures for the project, wanting to show her the beauty in nature beyond equations.

human.name: Ashley
human.description: Olivia Holland's roommate who befriends her during the difficult period after Olivia's breakup with Randy. Home alone during a thunderstorm watching The Walking Dead when mysterious knocking occurs. Helps Olivia recover from her breakup.

human.name: Abina
human.description: Team member on the Spheria Project who controls playback of the Spheria simulation recordings. Max commands Abina to pause playback during the final team review of the Polyan prayer circle and battle recordings.

human.name: Rick Grimes
human.description: Character from the TV show The Walking Dead that Ashley is watching during the thunderstorm in Chapter 31. Sneaks through a zombie-filled warehouse on screen. His on-screen actions are contrasted with the mysterious real-world knocking Ashley hears.

human.name: Randy
human.description: Engineering student in Boston, Olivia's long-distance boyfriend of four years. Uses a borrowed Seek Thermal Imager to spy on Ashley in the shower, is kicked out by Olivia, then breaks up with her by email and asks for his device back. His abandoned thermal imager becomes the pivotal tool Olivia uses to crack Dana's keypad.

human.name: Lee
human.description: Fast typist on the Spheria team, described as resembling a Buddha-like figure. Suggests Max ask Olivia to help with open house decorations rather than doing it himself.

polyan.name: Le·ma
polyan.description: A young Polyan with swirling patterns on her body (female marking). Selected to become apprentice to Sa·ma, the Lumen Master. Demonstrates quick thinking and problem-solving ability.

polyan.name: Sa·ma
polyan.description: The Colony's Lumen Master with straight textured lines (male marking). Guides Le·ma through dangerous trials. Possesses an artifact sensor with three triangles that gives him extraordinary abilities including controlling Zalisks.

polyan.name: Old Lumen Master
polyan.description: Feeble elder who lived in the Lumen Grotto. Selected and trained Sa·ma when he was young. Explained the testing process and purpose of Lumen Seekers.

polyan.name: Ti·ni
polyan.description: Young Drone who shows less fear of higher castes than others. Appears curious and respectful toward Sa·ma, asking questions during his storytelling.

polyan.name: Po·ni
polyan.description: Another young Drone who forgets her manners in her curiosity about Sa·ma's cave discovery. Stands admiringly next to Ti·ni.

polyan.name: Fa·ro
polyan.description: The first artificially engineered seven-legged Polyan god, created by the Council of Leaders. Ambitious and power-hungry, he stages a coup by eliminating the Council to become sole ruler of the Colony. Commands the Soldiers and enslaves the Workers to build a massive tower reaching toward the Source. Possesses an artifact device that lets him scan for living creatures and control Zalisk herds.

polyan.name: Ga·zo
polyan.description: Fa·ro's primary loyalist and enforcer. Convinced the Council to create a second god as backup for Fa·ro. Visits Sa·ma in prison to confirm he's alive. Leads the Soldier army in pursuit of the Workers.

polyan.name: Za·zo
polyan.description: Soldier who supports Fa·ro's leadership and accompanies Fa·ro and Ga·zo on the expedition to the golden cave. Part of Fa·ro's inner circle along with Ga·zo.

polyan.name: Hi·ma
polyan.description: One of Sa·ma's key lieutenants in the rebellion. Follows Sa·ma's secret instructions to gather twelve lead Workers at a hidden meeting place. Witnesses the tower collapse into the bridge across the Rift from inside the fallen structure.

polyan.name: Pu·ma
polyan.description: Accompanies Hi·ma following Sa·ma's secret instructions to the hidden meeting place for the Worker rebellion.

earth.name: Hôtel de Ville de Bruxelles
earth.description: Tower building in Brussels, Belgium where 25th Solvay Conference on Physics took place. Has balcony overlooking city.

earth.name: UConn Health Center
earth.description: Medical-dental school in Hartford County built 1970 on highest point. Expanded in 90s for clinical research. Houses Spheria Project. Contains Lyman Maynard Stowe Library with 'Spheres' sculptures.

earth.name: Spheria Project Office
earth.description: Research facility with makeshift lobby/reception desk, cubicles, conference room, kitchen. Team holds happy hour gatherings. Min works at reception desk with three large monitors.

earth.name: Graham's Airship 'Buruwa Gunya'
earth.description: A massive yellow luxury airship belonging to Graham, moored to the UConn Health Center roof via a disguised cell tower antenna. Features a suspended gondola with open floor plan, sunken booths, kitchen with stainless steel appliances, slanted wraparound windows, and private rooms toward the rear.

earth.name: Electric Boat Pier 3, Groton CT
earth.description: Submarine manufacturing facility at Shaw Cove with docks for Virginia-class submarines. Features a large yellow crane for lifting cargo and containers, minimally lit at night. Used for clandestine meetings.

earth.name: Secret red server room
earth.description: Hidden duplicate server room beneath the Qube floor with red lighting, containing unauthorized Tor network equipment and Gridway distributed computing system for DARPA weapons project

earth.name: Hospital incinerator chamber
earth.description: Domed propane-fueled incinerator where Min and Max become trapped; contains exhaust vent leading to chimney with clean-out door

earth.name: Lyman Maynard Stowe Library
earth.description: Library at UConn Health Center with 'Spheres' sculptures by Wopo Holop outside the Academic Entrance. Site where Jill committed suicide by jumping from the roof, landing on a sculpture pedestal near Tim. Has a cobblestone circle entry.

earth.name: Max's Apartment
earth.description: Max's minimalist apartment near UConn Health Center. Features an impressive entertainment center with video game systems and a bedroom with walls covered in bookcases. Min visits here to play Minecraft and have dinner dates with Max.

earth.name: UConn Health Center Rooftop
earth.description: Rooftop of the UConn Health Center building, accessed via emergency stairwell. Features a mooring mast disguised as a cell tower antenna, used as a docking point for Graham's airship 'Buruwa Gunya.'

earth.name: Quantum Server Room
earth.description: The main computer room where the Spheria experiment is hosted. It contains a build of the proprietary Qube based quantum computer, consisting of a traditional server rack plus two walls panels of Qubes, each emitting the glow of their alignment.

earth.name: Red Server Room
earth.description: Bundle of red cables and unauthorized Tor network/Gridway software running in secret duplicate server room beneath the Qube floor
earth.significance: Evidence of Dana's secret DARPA weapons project and unauthorized military collaboration

earth.name: The Exchange Apartment
earth.description: Olivia and Ashley's modern apartment building directly across from the UConn Health Center. A pub is located in the building. Olivia lives here with Ashley; Randy visits from Boston.

earth.name: 6th Floor Storage Room
earth.description: A dim, disorganized storage room one floor below the Spheria lab. Filled with filing cabinets, moving boxes, and office supplies. Contains a hidden metal door secured by a keypad — the entrance to Dana's secret server room. Code: 2465.

earth.name: The Butterfly Lounge
earth.description: A packed bar/lounge where Olivia and Ashley drink scorpion bowls to process the breakup. Has a DJ and a karaoke setup. Ashley signs them up for 'Stop Draggin' My Heart Around.'

spheria.name: Polyan Colony
spheria.description: Home settlement of the Polyans. Contains hives (igloo-like pointed structures). Center gathering place. Location where Drones, Builders, Soldiers, and Leaders live.

spheria.name: Uplift Pass
spheria.description: Large slit/crevice in blue mountains containing platforms that allow passage to other side. Contains steps. Named by ancestors. Used for Lumen Seeker training.

spheria.name: Valley of Three
spheria.description: Large perfect triangle formation of mountains on opposite hemisphere with no rivers inside. Used for navigation at night. Legends say gods play games there (flickering dots sometimes visible).

spheria.name: Red Rivers
spheria.description: Rivers of red liquid/energy that flow through Spherian landscape. Can be crossed with bridges made from brown rock. Illuminate blue mountains at night.

spheria.name: Lumen Grotto
spheria.description: Hexagonal room cut into hill-like formation far from Colony. Resting place where old Lumen Master waits to test potential Lumen Seekers. Contains glowing yellow crystal.

spheria.name: Fertile Field
spheria.description: Legendary location of endless energy. Not always visible/present. Sa·ma discovered it and received artifact sensor there. Considered a gift from the gods.

spheria.name: The Golden Cave
spheria.description: A mysterious cave discovered by Sa·ma containing golden crystalline formations, strange glowing plants, and a portal to the 'Qubessence.' Features twisting maze-like passages with light-producing plants that can be broken to create beams. Contains a room with a portal that Sa·ma encounters a cave god through.

spheria.name: Council Building Prison Cell
spheria.description: Triangular chamber tapering to a point, the hollow interior of the Council building. Originally a shelter against Zalisk attacks, later used for storage, now repurposed as the Polyans' first prison. Features a single large door, no windows, and a shallow hexagonal pit in the center.

spheria.name: The bridge (fallen tower)
spheria.description: The massive tower knocked down by Sa·ma's cannon to span across the Rift, creating the first bridge to the forbidden side

spheria.name: Fa·ro's Chamber
spheria.description: The apex chamber of the tallest structure in the Colony, formerly the Council chamber, now Fa·ro's throne room after he eliminated the Council

objects.name: Violet Core Crystal
objects.description: Life-giving crystal at center of all Spherian beings. Powers their existence - without it they are inanimate tetrahedron structures. Large ones found in Zalisks are valuable.
objects.significance: Fundamental to all Spherian life; object of Polyan hunts

objects.name: Green Crystals
objects.description: Buried crystals containing seed energy that Zalisks forage for by digging with tusks.
objects.significance: Food source for herbivorous Spherian creatures

objects.name: Yellow Shards/Crystals
objects.description: Small yellow crystals that provide light and energy. Can be dropped into Rift where they eventually transform into rising bubbles. Found in Lumen Grotto.
objects.significance: Energy source for Polyans; used in rituals and tests

objects.name: Blue Mountain Crystals
objects.description: Blue crystals that emit repulsive force field, making them impossible to touch. Form impassable mountain ranges that divide the world.
objects.significance: Natural barriers that shape geography and limit exploration

objects.name: Artifact Sensor
objects.description: Special indigo sensor with three triangles pointing in different directions. Attached to Sa·ma's core. Gives extraordinary abilities including commanding Zalisks.
objects.significance: Divine gift from Fertile Field; passed from master to apprentice; enhances Lumenary abilities

objects.name: Brown Rock Blocks
objects.description: Loose inert brown rock material pushed by Drones and stacked by Builders to construct hives and bridges.
objects.significance: Primary construction material in Spherian world

objects.name: Spy Glasses
objects.description: Thick-rimmed glasses from spygear.com that Min wears constantly. Record everything she sees and hears.
objects.significance: Allow Min to document her experiences; she films her interview

objects.name: VR Headset and 3D SpaceMouse
objects.description: Virtual reality equipment with glove that allows team to observe and navigate inside Spheria. SpaceMouse looks like truck towing ball with programmable buttons.
objects.significance: Primary interface for observing Spherian world; only engineers allowed to interact

objects.name: 'Spheres' Sculptures
objects.description: Art installations by Wopo Holop at UConn library entrance. Include bronze objects: human retina, dividing frog eggs, Earth, acorn, DNA strand, faceted and smooth spheres.
objects.significance: Location of Jill's suicide; thematic connection to project

objects.name: Seven-sided Core Crystal
objects.description: Empty crystal structure used in the creation of seven-legged Polyans/gods, surrounded by seven orange elongated crystals near each facet
objects.significance: Central component in the ritual to create another god to accompany Fa·ro, representing the Polyans' attempt to control or replicate divine creation

objects.name: Blue Rock Wall
objects.description: Defensive wall constructed along the red river to protect Colony hives from destruction
objects.significance: One of Sa·ma's insightful designs demonstrating his engineering abilities and value to the Colony despite his imprisonment

objects.name: Glowing Cave Plants
objects.description: Strange plants in the golden cave that produce light and can be broken to create directional light beams; they heal themselves over time
objects.significance: Mark pathways through the cave maze and can be manipulated to reveal or conceal the portal to the Qubessence

objects.name: Yellow Shard
objects.description: Small glowing crystal used as portable light source
objects.significance: Provides minimal illumination in Sa·ma's prison cell, highlighting his reduced circumstances

objects.name: Sa·ma's beam cannon
objects.description: Device created by Sa·ma that fires energy beams capable of destroying tower support columns and launching Polyans through the air
objects.significance: Revolutionary technology that enables the Workers' rebellion by creating the bridge across the Rift and functioning as a weapon

objects.name: Fa·ro's artifact
objects.description: Device that allows Fa·ro to see through obstructions and control Zalisk; stolen by Le·ma during battle
objects.significance: Key to controlling the Zalisk herd and turning the tide of battle against the Soldiers

objects.name: Incinerator gurney with corpse
objects.description: Metal medical gurney with embalmed cadaver used as makeshift ladder inside the incinerator chamber
objects.significance: Provides escape route for Min and Max through exhaust vent to chimney

objects.name: Blue rock barrier wall
objects.description: Long continuous vertical wall of blue crystalline material creating a clearing perpendicular to the Soldiers' path
objects.significance: Defensive fortification created by Workers to slow Soldier pursuit and protect their escape route

objects.name: Dana's Confession Video
objects.description: Pre-recorded video of Dana confessing to murdering Olivia Holland, locking Max in the incinerator, and making a deal with DARPA for military funding and personal land in Costa Rica. Played at the board presentation to expose Dana's crimes.
objects.significance: Max's insurance policy ensuring Dana's crimes would be exposed regardless of outcome; directly leads to Dana's arrest

objects.name: Virginia-class Submarine Photonics Mast
objects.description: Low-profile mast with camera array connected by fiber optic cables, resembling a traditional periscope without hull penetration. Observed at Electric Boat Pier 3 in Groton, CT during the clandestine meeting.
objects.significance: Advanced U.S. Navy submarine technology present at the mysterious pier meeting, suggesting military involvement

objects.name: 'A Short History of Nearly Everything'
objects.description: Non-fiction book by Bill Bryson with a blue cover featuring a small Earth photo and asterisk. Max's all-time favorite book, read at least once a year. He wishes for an updated edition covering quantum physics.
objects.significance: Reveals Max's passion for understanding the universe and his belief that they are creating history with the Spheria project

objects.name: Keurig Coffee Machine
objects.description: Coffee maker in the Spheria Project office kitchen with yellow blinking LEDs, uses Dark Embers k-cups. Min uses it during late-night work sessions.
objects.significance: Recurring companion during Min's late-night preparations before Graham's visit; her computer crashes after she returns from making coffee

objects.name: Mooring Mast
objects.description: Structure on the UConn Health Center roof that appears to be a cell tower antenna but functions as a docking point for Graham's luxury airship. Was built as a stipulation of Graham's funding grant.
objects.significance: Enables Graham to arrive by airship directly to the facility; demonstrates his eccentric wealth and the scale of his investment in the project

objects.name: Gridway Distributed Computing System
objects.description: Open-source meta-scheduling software enabling large-scale sharing of computing resources across distributed processors. Found running on Dana's secret server, being used for unauthorized weapons calculations.
objects.significance: Key evidence of the DARPA weapons project; its presence in the secret server room proves Dana is conducting large-scale unauthorized computing operations

objects.name: Tor Network
objects.description: Navy-developed anonymous communication system later adopted for dark web use. Running on Dana's secret server to provide untraceable internet communication despite no official internet access in the facility.
objects.significance: Enables covert DARPA weapons data transmission; discovered by Min using Olivia's password and constitutes evidence of Dana's unauthorized military collaboration

objects.name: Orange Elongated Crystals
objects.description: Seven crystals placed near each facet of the core during the god creation ritual.
objects.significance: Part of the god-creation process; positioned by Leaders during ceremony

objects.name: Seek Thermal Imager
objects.description: A phone camera attachment belonging to Randy that detects infrared heat signatures. Originally borrowed from his engineering lab. He uses it to spy on Ashley; Olivia keeps it after kicking him out. She later uses it to read the residual heat on the keypad buttons, identifying the code 2465.
objects.significance: The pivotal object of Olivia's Log — Randy's betrayal connects directly to Olivia's discovery of Dana's secret. A tool of voyeurism that becomes a tool of espionage.

objects.name: Olivia's Journal / Log File
objects.description: A digital journal Olivia maintains on her work computer. After discovering Dana's secret, she adds a coded entry using an acrostic cipher — the first letter/number of each sentence spells out 'FLOOR 6 CODE 2465.' Intended as insurance sent to Ashley by email.
objects.significance: Olivia's attempt to leave a trail and protect herself. The email fails to send, making it a tragic near-miss. Gives the novella its title — Olivia's Log.

objects.name: Red Qubes
objects.description: Server hardware components that are normally incinerated after use in the Spheria project. Dana has been diverting them to a secret 6th floor server room where every Qube glows red. Connected to an outside network, they run complex unsolved equations for Dana's benefactor.
objects.significance: The physical evidence of Dana's corruption. Their red glow mirrors the red rock rivers of Spheria — an aesthetic parallel between the two worlds.

objects.name: Keypad (6th Floor Metal Door)
objects.description: A numeric keypad securing the hidden server room door on the 6th floor. Code is 2465, deduced by Olivia using the thermal imager to read residual heat on the most-pressed keys.
objects.significance: The literal and symbolic lock on Dana's secret.

objects.name: Gris-Gris
objects.description: A small leather pouch on a string necklace, sewn shut, containing a couple of small solid objects whose exact contents are never revealed. Worn around the neck.
objects.significance: A common token shared among the Spheria project team. Ravi and at least two other unnamed team members also wear one, suggesting Abina gave them to the broader team.

factions.name: Polyan Castes
factions.description: Social hierarchy with specialized roles: 3-legged Drones (workers), 4-legged Builders (construction), 5-legged Soldiers (hunters/warriors), 6-legged Leaders (governance). Females have swirling patterns, males have straight lines.

factions.name: Lumen Seekers
factions.description: Elite group who possess special knowledge about the world. Only confident Polyans who venture far from Colony can find Lumen Grotto and be tested. Lumen Master has status equal to or higher than Leaders.

factions.name: Spheria Project Team
factions.description: Scientists at UConn Health Center studying virtual ecosystem with computer-generated creatures called Spherians/Polyans. Mix of physicists, programmers, and researchers. Hold happy hour gatherings.

factions.name: Fa·ro's Faction
factions.description: Growing authoritarian movement centered on Fa·ro as a decisive god-leader who breaks Council gridlock. Represents shift from democratic consensus to autocratic rule, with Ga·zo as primary enforcer.

factions.name: Rebellious Workers
factions.description: All Workers in the Colony who secretly organized under Sa·ma's leadership to flee across the Rift using the fallen tower as a bridge, escaping Fa·ro's tyranny

factions.name: DARPA partnership
factions.description: Secret military collaboration arranged by Dana with Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency to weaponize the Spheria technology in exchange for funding and personal land in Costa Rica

factions.name: Dana's Shadow Project
factions.description: A clandestine operation run by Dana Carter using diverted red Qubes on the 6th floor. Dana solves complex equations and manipulates Polyans inside Spheria to change their moral alignment for an unnamed outside benefactor. Operates entirely without Max's knowledge. Discovered by intern Olivia Holland, who was murdered to prevent exposure.

factions.name: Council Leaders
factions.description: The eight six-legged Polyans who govern the Colony through majority vote. Gridlocked and often indecisive, they increasingly deferred to Fa·ro before he eliminated them entirely and declared himself sole ruler. They attempted to create a second seven-legged god as a backup to Fa·ro, which was the stated reason Fa·ro gave for their deaths.

blue_crystal.description: Blue crystal mountains emit a repulsive force - Polyans cannot touch them directly. The repulsion allows floating above blue surfaces, used to climb structures like the Uplift Steps through pendulum motion. Blue rock can be shaped into barriers and structures.

red_crystal.description: Red rock rivers ATTRACT everything. Living things with violet cores stick to red surfaces but can detach with effort. Non-living objects touching red turn red themselves and sink into the river. When the Source turns red, rivers widen and spread - a natural disaster.

brown_crystal.description: Brown rock is neutral terrain - the standard ground material. It doesn't repel or attract. Brown blocks are used as building materials. Brown rock can be overturned to reveal buried green crystals beneath.

green_crystal.description: Green polyhedrons are plants that grow on brown rock terrain. Some form arched shapes large enough to walk under. Plants have no violet core, so they turn red and are consumed when touching red rivers. Inside caves, glowing green plants emit light and can be broken to create light beams.

violet_crystal.description: The violet crystal core is the source of life energy for all living creatures in Spheria. Without it, beings are mere inanimate structures of connected tetrahedrons. Cores pulse with light and store energy drained from other sources.

the_source.description: Gravity pushes outward from the center toward the sphere's inside surface where Polyans live. The Source itself generates this repulsion which gets stronger the closer ('higher') you go. Technically, objects can never reach the source because the repulsion grows to 100%.
the_source.notes: Although humans can watch Spheria from any vantage point, the Source is a default camera position that can zoom into any area of the world surface.

bubbles.description: When the Source is lit, it emits one of several possible colors (see the colors tab.). Each color regulates the current behavior or priorities of the inhabitants of Spheria. The color changes when a bubble of a given color rises from the rift and makes contact.
bubbles.notes: Bubbles originate deep in the rift and rise, about once per day. The origin of bubbles is currently unknown.

the_rift.description: The Rift is an uncrossable chasm dividing the world into two hemispheres. It appears as a vertical artificial horizon. Bubbles of different colors rise slowly from the Rift, giving warning before they reach the Source and trigger their effects. Below the Rift lies Qubessence, the afterlife realm.

source_colors.effect: Normal behavior - compels creatures to forage and hunt for energy
source_colors.triggered_by: Default state when no colored bubble has reached the Source

source_colors.effect: Growth - vegetation grows new branches and spreads
source_colors.triggered_by: A green bubble rising from the Rift and hitting the Source

source_colors.effect: Breeding/reproduction - creatures capable of reproduction begin to procreate
source_colors.triggered_by: A violet bubble rising from the Rift and hitting the Source

source_colors.effect: Mountains grow and spread - natural disaster as blue tetrahedrons sprout on open faces
source_colors.triggered_by: A blue bubble rising from the Rift and hitting the Source

source_colors.effect: Rivers widen and spread - natural disaster equivalent
source_colors.triggered_by: A red bubble rising from the Rift and hitting the Source

source_colors.effect: Rest/abundance - all life crystals fill from within, no hunting needed
source_colors.triggered_by: A yellow bubble rising from the Rift, signaled by Lumen Seeker throwing yellow shard into Rift

geography.name: World Shape
geography.description: Hollow sphere - Polyans live on the inside surface with the Source floating at the center

geography.name: The Source
geography.description: The bright shining orb above the Polyan world that governs behavior and provides light. Changes color when bubbles rising from the rift contact it (see Source Colors for these effects.) Fades to create a night/sleep cycle).

geography.name: Blue Mountains
geography.description: Mountain ranges made of blue crystal that emit repulsive force, making them impassable. Divide world into maze-like sections. Nearly vertical on one side.

geography.name: Red Rivers
geography.description: Rivers made of red crystal that emit attractive force, making them impassable. Divide world into maze-like sections. Nearly vertical on one side.

geography.name: The Rift
geography.description: Massive chasm at edge of world. Appears as vertical artificial horizon. So deep the bottom cannot be seen. Yellow shards dropped in eventually become bubbles rising back up.

caste_system.role: Menial tasks - pushing blocks, making rhythmic sounds at celebrations, foraging for buried green crystals
caste_system.social_rank: Lowest - eat last and sometimes get nothing
caste_system.behaviors: Most numerous caste, perform simple labor, dig for green seed crystals in the ground

caste_system.social_rank: Third in hierarchy - eat third after Leaders and Soldiers
caste_system.behaviors: Build structures, construct hives, handle materials, capable of organized rebellion

caste_system.social_rank: Second in hierarchy - eat second after Leaders
caste_system.behaviors: Hunt Zalisks in coordinated teams, use whips as weapons, enforce Colony rules, can be aggressive

caste_system.role: Governance and wisdom - make decisions by majority vote
caste_system.social_rank: Highest natural caste - eat first, just one leg short of a god
caste_system.behaviors: There are exactly 8 Leaders: Wu·sa, Yo·sa, Me·sa, Ju·sa, Ki·sa, Co·sa, Pi·sa, and Vu·sa. They govern by majority vote; tie means no action taken. They meet in the Council Chamber atop the Council structure.

caste_system.social_rank: Above all castes - revered as divine
caste_system.behaviors: Gods appear as 7-legged Polyans with colored cores. No Polyan is born naturally with 7 legs until the Leaders engineered Fa·ro, the first artificially created god.

energy.how_they_feed: Polyans feed by touching their core's lower point to a violet shard and draining energy into themselves. Primary food source is energy harvested from Zalisks - drain the beast's violet core using clear crystal shards as conduits, then consume the stored violet energy.
energy.crystal_colors: Violet crystals contain life energy (highest quality). Green crystals buried in ground contain seed energy (lower quality food source foraged by Drones). Clear crystals serve as conduits for energy transfer.
energy.hierarchy_of_feeding: Leaders eat first, Soldiers second, Workers third, Drones last (sometimes getting nothing). When a Polyan's violet core runs empty, they die.
energy.death_state: When dead, a Polyan's physical structure (polygon shell) falls apart. Their 'structure' descends into the Rift to Qubessence, the afterlife realm.

sensors.description: Polyans have indigo sensors on their cores - each sensor has a specific function and can be a vacant port awaiting an attachment
sensors.types: Eye sensors for vision, ear sensors for hearing, mouth sensors for speech. Sensors can be vacant 'ports' waiting to be filled.
sensors.artifact_sensor: Sa·ma possesses a three-sided indigo rod with tetrahedrons on each end, given by god Pi·ro. It attaches to a vacant sensor port and grants ability to see beyond normal perception, sense life forms and structures through obstructions (violet outlines for living, yellow for objects of focus), and control/command creatures like Zalisks.

overview.biology: Polyans are comprised of connected tetrahedrons forming a roundish body. They look like multi-legged spiders with legs evenly spaced around their body rather than lined up on sides. Each has a violet crystal core at center that pulses with light. Their body has textured patterns.
overview.gender: Polyans have male and female genders. Males have straight textured line patterns on their bodies; females have swirling patterns. Ca·ro (green plant goddess) is explicitly female.
overview.naming_convention: Names follow pattern of two syllables separated by a dot: Sa·ma, Le·ma, Fa·ro, Ga·zo, etc. Leaders all end in ·sa: Wu·sa, Yo·sa, Me·sa, Ju·sa, Ki·sa, Co·sa, Pi·sa, Vu·sa. Gods end in ·ro: Ra·ju (creator), Ta·ro, Ke·ro, Pi·ro, Ju·ro, Ca·ro, Na·ro, Wi·ro, Su·ro.

gods.name: Ra·ju
gods.domain: Creator of all - the original god with 8 legs
gods.role_in_creation: Created the entire world. His 8 children detached as his legs and became the other gods.

gods.name: Ta·ro
gods.role_in_creation: Created the brown rock terrain and solid ground

gods.name: Ke·ro
gods.role_in_creation: Created living creatures and the spark of life

gods.name: Pi·ro
gods.role_in_creation: Created the red rivers. Hot-tempered god who decreed everything touching red turns red. Gave Sa·ma the artifact sensor.

gods.name: Ju·ro
gods.role_in_creation: Created the blue crystal mountains that divide the world into sections

gods.name: Ca·ro
gods.role_in_creation: Female goddess who created all plant life and green crystals

gods.name: Na·ro
gods.role_in_creation: Created the light of the Source that illuminates the world

gods.name: Wi·ro
gods.role_in_creation: Created the ability for creatures to reproduce and multiply

gods.name: Su·ro
gods.role_in_creation: Created the sensory organs - eyes, ears, mouths - that allow perception

afterlife.name: Qubessence
afterlife.description: The afterlife realm below the Rift. Said to be a Violet landscape with violet mountains and violet sky. Described as a place of endless energy where there is no need to hunt.

afterlife.name: How to Reach
afterlife.description: Only structures (dead Polyans) can enter Qubessence. When a Polyan dies, their structure is said to descend into the Rift.

afterlife.name: Warning
afterlife.description: Living Polyans who jump into the Rift are destroyed agonizingly - Qubessence only accepts structures (dead bodies), not living bodies with active cores. This secret is kept by Lumen Seekers.

lumen_tradition.lumen_master: The Colony's spiritual and knowledge leader, publicly known role. Sa·ma holds this position. Has special status equal to or higher than six-legged Leaders. Teaches apprentices and preserves sacred knowledge.
lumen_tradition.lumen_seeker: Apprentice in training to eventually become Lumen Master. Le·ma is the current Seeker. Learns sacred rituals, secret passages, and hidden knowledge from the Master.
lumen_tradition.lumen_walk: Rite of passage where apprentice travels with Master across dangerous proving ground to the Rift, performs offering ritual (throwing yellow shard into Rift to trigger yellow bubble/abundance), then must find their own way back alone as a test.
lumen_tradition.lumen_elder: Retired Lumen Master who goes into seclusion and trains the new Seeker when the current Master is too busy. Do·ma was the previous elder. Lives in the Lumen Grotto.
lumen_tradition.lumen_grotto: Hidden hexagonal room with a glowing yellow crystal at center. Secret dwelling where Lumen Elder lives in seclusion, far from the Colony. Serves as meeting place and sanctuary.

legends.name: Creation Story
legends.description: Ra·ju (8-legged creator god with white core) created the world. His 8 children each detached as one of his legs and changed color to become the other gods: Ta·ro (brown) created land/earth, Ke·ro (orange) created life and creatures, Pi·ro (red) created rivers and red rock, Ju·ro (blue) created mountains and divisions, Ca·ro (green, female) created plants and vegetation, Na·ro (yellow) created light and the Source's glow, Wi·ro (violet) created multiplication and reproduction, Su·ro (indigo) created senses.

legends.name: The Fertile Field
legends.description: A legendary circle of 20 large violet crystals that replenish themselves with endless energy. Sa·ma discovered it actually exists - it appears and disappears, not always in the same location. The crystals provide abundant life energy without need to hunt.

legends.name: Valley of Three
legends.description: A large perfect triangle formation of mountains on the opposite hemisphere with no rivers inside. A fixture in the night sky that Soldiers use for navigation. Legend says gods go there to play games. Sometimes flickering dots (gods) can be seen moving inside on certain nights. No Polyan has ever reached it.

legends.name: Troaten
legends.description: Mythological massive creature with many tentacles that camouflages itself and spins defensively, piercing cores of prey at its center. In mythology, the Troaten became the Source and the Rift.

legends.name: The Portal Cave
legends.description: A cave with twisting passages filled with glowing green plants that can be broken to create light beams. Deep inside is a room where a seven-legged god (cave god) guards a portal. The portal leads to Qubessence and glows violet. Contains streets that appear to be made of gold.

rituals.name: Yellow Shard Offering
rituals.description: Sacred ritual performed during Lumen Walk where the Lumen Seeker throws a yellow shard into the Rift. This triggers a yellow bubble to rise, which signals a day of abundance when all life crystals fill from within.

rituals.name: God Creation Ceremony
rituals.description: The Leaders gather in Council Chamber around an empty seven-sided core crystal. Seven orange elongated crystals are placed near each facet. When the Source turns violet (breeding season), the Leaders channel energy to create a seven-legged Polyan god. This is how Fa·ro was created.

rituals.name: Celebratory Gathering
rituals.description: Colony members gather at the central dais in the Colony center. Drones make rhythmic sounds. A platform/staging area is used for performances and announcements.

rituals.name: Prayer Circle
rituals.description: Polyans form a circle and appear to pray together - the first observed religious behavior showing organized worship rather than just acknowledging gods.

rituals.name: Council Voting
rituals.description: Eight Leaders meet in columned Council Chamber atop Council structure. Guards stationed at three faces prevent approach during sessions. Leaders govern by majority vote; tie votes result in no action (later Fa·ro breaks ties, then seizes absolute power).

fauna.name: Zalisk
fauna.description: Enormous six-legged beast with five scooping tusks on a shelf arrangement and a massive violet core at center
fauna.behavior: Aggressive creature that forages for buried green crystals. Known for severe aggression - many Polyans died before learning to hunt them as coordinated teams. Uses tusks to overturn chunks of brown rock to find crystals.
fauna.danger_level: Extremely high - primary threat to Polyans, requires team of 9 Soldiers to safely hunt
fauna.energy_role: Primary food source for Polyans - their enormous violet cores provide substantial life energy when drained

fauna.name: Troaten
fauna.description: Massive creature with many tentacles that camouflages itself
fauna.behavior: Spins defensively and pierces the cores of prey at its center using tentacles
fauna.danger_level: Extreme - mythologically powerful enough to become the Source and Rift
fauna.energy_role: Unknown - appears in mythology more than current ecosystem

flora.name: Green Polyhedron Plants
flora.description: Plants shaped as green polyhedrons that grow on brown rock terrain. Some form large arched shapes you can walk under.
flora.properties: Have no violet core, so turn red and are consumed when touching red rivers. Different from green crystals buried in ground.
flora.uses: Provide visual cover and shelter for Polyans moving through wilderness

flora.name: Glowing Cave Plants
flora.description: Green plants inside caves that emit light, found in the portal cave's twisting passages
flora.properties: Can be broken to create directional light beams. Will heal themselves over time if broken.
flora.uses: Provide illumination in dark caves and can be used to reveal hidden passages when broken

flora.name: Green Seed Crystals
flora.description: Small green crystals buried beneath the brown rock surface
flora.properties: Contain seed energy - a lower quality food source compared to violet crystals
flora.uses: Foraged by Drones and lower castes, eaten by creatures like Zalisks. Emergency food when violet energy unavailable.

crystals.found_in: Centers of all living creatures as their core. The Fertile Field contains 20 large self-replenishing violet crystals. Qubessence afterlife is entirely violet.
crystals.properties: Stores and pulses with life energy. When drained, the creature dies. Can be refilled by draining energy from other violet sources.
crystals.significance: The source of all life in Spheria - without a violet core, beings are just inanimate structures

crystals.found_in: Mountains and mountain ranges that divide the world. Can be shaped into barriers and walls.
crystals.properties: Emits repulsive force - prevents direct contact. Repulsion allows floating/gliding above surfaces. Can be used to build walls and structures.
crystals.significance: Creates natural barriers dividing world into sections. When Source turns blue, mountains grow and spread as a natural disaster.

crystals.found_in: Rivers of red rock that flow through the landscape. Red pipes in hidden facilities.
crystals.properties: Attracts everything - living things with cores stick but can detach; non-living objects turn red and sink. When Source turns red, rivers widen.
crystals.significance: Major geographical feature and hazard. Can trap and consume Polyans if they're not careful.

crystals.found_in: Small shards found in the Proving Ground near the Rift. Large yellow crystal in the Lumen Grotto. Streets in portal cave appear gold/yellow.
crystals.properties: Used in offering ritual - thrown into Rift to trigger yellow bubble for abundance. The Grotto crystal glows and provides sanctuary.
crystals.significance: Sacred color associated with rest, abundance, and the Lumen tradition. Signals rare days when all life crystals fill from within.

crystals.found_in: Buried in brown rock ground, vegetation/plants
crystals.properties: Contains seed energy - lower quality than violet. Plants have green color but no cores.
crystals.significance: Supplementary food source for lower castes and foraging creatures

crystals.found_in: Standard ground terrain throughout the world
crystals.properties: Neutral - doesn't attract or repel. Used as building material in blocks.
crystals.significance: The foundation terrain where most life exists and structures are built

crystals.found_in: Elongated crystals used in god creation ceremony
crystals.properties: Seven orange crystals placed around the seven-sided core during the ritual to create a seven-legged god
crystals.significance: Essential component in engineering divine beings

crystals.found_in: Sensors on Polyan cores. Sa·ma's artifact is an indigo rod.
crystals.properties: Forms sensory organs - eyes, ears, mouths. Artifact version grants enhanced perception, ability to see through obstructions, and command creatures.
crystals.significance: Associated with Su·ro, god of senses. Enables perception and consciousness.

crystals.found_in: Used by hunters as energy conduits
crystals.properties: Serves as conduit for transferring violet energy from killed Zalisks to storage
crystals.significance: Essential tool for harvesting life energy from hunted beasts

book1.title: Spheria 1: Eversion
book1.subtitle: Eversion
synopsis: Spheria follows parallel narratives on Earth and within a revolutionary virtual ecosystem simulation. Physicist Max recruits intern Min to join the Spheria Project after a previous intern's suspicious suicide, working alongside a team that observes crystalline beings called Polyans living inside their digital creation.

Inside the simulation, young Le·ma apprentices under the wise Lumen Master Sa·ma, who leads her on a perilous journey through Uplift Pass to discover the legendary Fertile Field beyond the blue crystal mountains. During their quest, Sa·ma acquires a mysterious triangular artifact that grants him power to control creatures, saving Le·ma from a deadly Zalisk beast. As their final test, Le·ma must find an impossible route back over the mountains.

The Earth scientists observe these developments through VR equipment, grappling with their role as creators watching their simulated world evolve. Tensions escalate when Polyan society enters upheaval with unusual behaviors emerging alongside four threatening red bubbles near their Colony.

Sa·ma discovers a golden cave containing a portal to the Qubessence, the realm of gods, but power-hungry Fa·ro forces him to reveal this secret location, resulting in Sa·ma becoming the first imprisoned Polyan. The Council increasingly defers to Fa·ro's authority, eventually deciding to create a second seven-legged god.

Meanwhile, the Spheria Project gains media attention through a Gizmodo article as Max and Min's relationship deepens. Project leader Graham arrives for a critical funding review, but Min's computer mysteriously crashes before essential calculations are complete. Covert activities at a submarine pier hint at external threats to the project's integrity.

The narrative reaches its climax when Min discovers that team member Dana is the true villain who murdered previous intern Olivia and orchestrated attacks on Min and Max to conceal her secret DARPA weapons deal exploiting Spheria technology. Dana traps Min and Max in a hospital incinerator, but volunteer Walter helps them escape.

Simultaneously, Fa·ro establishes absolute dictatorship in Spheria after eliminating the Council. Sa·ma leads a brilliant Worker rebellion, using a beam cannon to topple a tower across the Rift, creating a bridge to freedom. Le·ma steals Fa·ro's artifact and weaponizes it to control Zalisk creatures who massacre Fa·ro's Soldiers.

Dana's video confession exposes her crimes to Graham and the team, and Max saves Captain Brennan from Dana's gun. The Workers successfully cross the Rift to achieve liberation while the human scientists restore integrity to their groundbreaking project, resolving both parallel stories of tyranny, rebellion, and ultimate freedom.

chapters.title: Shard of Life
chapters.synopsis: Nine Soldier-caste Polyans (five-limbed beings with violet cores) hunt a massive six-legged Zalisk beast for its violet life crystal. The Source governs their behavior. They work as coordinated team to take down the dangerous creature.

chapters.title: 1% Inspiration, 99% Funding
chapters.synopsis: Max, a physicist, stands on balcony in Brussels after presenting at prestigious 25th Solvay Conference on Physics. His friend David, conference chair, congratulates him. Max reflects on scientific heroes Einstein, Curie, and Poincaré.

chapters.title: Uplift Pass
chapters.synopsis: Le·ma follows Sa·ma on journey far from Colony to blue mountains. They enter Uplift Pass, a crevice through the mountains. Sa·ma reveals Le·ma is his apprentice Lumen Seeker and will someday replace him as Colony's Lumen Master.

chapters.title: Cell Division
chapters.synopsis: Timothy and Jill arrive at UConn Health Center library at night to study. As Tim ties his shoe near 'Spheres' sculptures, Jill commits suicide by jumping from above, her blood spattering his new white sneakers.

chapters.title: Arcane Physics
chapters.synopsis: Le·ma must descend steep blue mountain by bouncing off repulsive force field. After terrifying descent, she learns to glide using the field. Sa·ma teaches her about physics of their world and tests her problem-solving.

chapters.title: First Impressions
chapters.synopsis: Max waits for elevator, frustrated by kids who pressed all buttons. He philosophizes about elevator design while slowly ascending. He's late for interview with candidate to replace tragic previous intern.

chapters.title: The Interview
chapters.synopsis: Min interviews with Max for internship. Her roommate Sheila (Australian medical student) found the posting. Min films interview with spy glasses and later shows Sheila footage of the Spheria Project and its computer-generated Polyan creatures.

chapters.title: The Rift
chapters.synopsis: Le·ma and Sa·ma camp beside the massive Rift at world's edge. At night they observe Valley of Three and other hemisphere. Sa·ma explains nobody has reached opposite side. Le·ma drops yellow shard into bottomless Rift.

chapters.title: First Day
chapters.synopsis: Min starts work at reception desk with three monitors. Max introduces her to team at meeting. She learns about Spheria Project structure and meets colleagues. Project involves monitoring virtual world with Spherian creatures.

chapters.title: The Fertile Field
chapters.synopsis: Flashback: Young Sa·ma explored beyond Colony and found Lumen Grotto with old feeble Polyan beside yellow crystal. Elder explained this is test location - only confident Polyans venture far enough to find it and become Lumen Seekers.

chapters.title: Min's Assignment
chapters.synopsis: Min arrives at work with Starbucks coffee. Max summons her to office and browses his books while he prepares files. She notices 'A Short History of Nearly Everything' by Bill Bryson is his favorite, read yearly.

chapters.title: Return Journey
chapters.synopsis: Le·ma wakes to find Sa·ma gone - she must return alone as final test. She sees yellow bubble rising from Rift (her shard transformed). Travels back toward mountains, observing environment more carefully, encountering various Spherian life.

chapters.title: Happy Hour
chapters.synopsis: At 6:12pm, team gathers for happy hour with ethnic food. Min brings Vietnamese Goi Cuon (salad rolls) with Nuoc cham sauce. Tim makes frozen drinks. Team bonds over food and conversation about their work.

chapters.title: Extra Sense
chapters.synopsis: Zalisk attacks Le·ma but Sa·ma commands it to leave using mysterious power. He reveals new artifact sensor with three triangles on his core - gift from gods found at Fertile Field. Gives him greater insight and control over creatures.

chapters.title: Starlight Walk
chapters.synopsis: Max and Min leave office at night, Max intoxicated. They walk through parking lot. Max sits in unlocked Mercedes, briefly worrying Min. They discuss their experiences and the project under the night sky.

chapters.title: Cliffhanger
chapters.synopsis: Le·ma and Sa·ma head back toward vertical blue mountains. Sa·ma reveals there's no passage on this side - she must create her own way over using her knowledge. He begins guiding her to solve this final test of the Lumen Walk.

chapters.title: God from the Machine
chapters.synopsis: Jean uses VR headset and 3D SpaceMouse to observe Spheria from inside the Source. She watches Drones and Builders constructing new hive at Colony. She wants to help but is forbidden to interact - only engineers can manipulate the world.

chapters.title: Prime Directive
chapters.synopsis: Dana reviews weekly logs showing increased unusual Polyan behavior. For the first time, anthropology entries exceed engineering entries. Jean's notes indicate Polyans are moving toward a new social zeitgeist or 'new age,' with interesting behaviors happening off-camera.

chapters.title: Crossing Over
chapters.synopsis: Max drives to UConn Health Center and is amazed by Graham's massive yellow airship 'Buruwa Gunya' moored to the building's roof. He experiences the Ponzo Illusion observing the airship's size relative to the building through trees.

chapters.title: Running Out
chapters.synopsis: Dana and Max climb to the roof and board Graham's luxury airship. The interior features an open floor plan with sunken booths, kitchen, wraparound windows, and indicates Graham's arrival for an important meeting about the project.

chapters.title: Cat and Mouse
chapters.synopsis: A mysterious figure in a black sedan arrives at Electric Boat Pier 3 in Groton during a rainstorm. They meet near a Virginia-class submarine and cargo containers beneath a crane in darkness, suggesting clandestine activity related to the project.

chapters.title: In Our Image
chapters.synopsis: The eight Leaders (Wu·sa, Yo·sa, Me·sa, Ju·sa, Ki·sa, Co·sa, Pi·sa, Vu·sa) convene in the Council Chamber as the fourth consecutive red bubble rises. They debate moving the Colony as the expanding red river threatens dwellings. Sa·ma cannot explain why the gods are angry and dares not reveal his encounters with Pi·ro and Ju·ro.

chapters.title: Nourishment
chapters.synopsis: Max and Min have dinner and discuss religion. Min was raised Catholic after her parents converted upon emigrating, while Max believes the universe is mathematical and algorithmic, with God having created fundamental rules that allow everything to self-assemble over time.

chapters.title: Pro Creation
chapters.synopsis: Min visits Max's minimalist apartment featuring impressive entertainment center and video game systems. They bond over Minecraft and discuss the game's appeal of building your own story. Min is surprised by Max's healthy refrigerator selections.

chapters.title: Start the Press
chapters.synopsis: A Gizmodo article by Moses Diego titled 'Scientists Play God In This Virtual Experiment' is published. The article describes the Polyan creatures, their caste system based on leg count, and the seven-legged Polyan revered as a god, bringing public attention to the project.

chapters.title: Conspiracy
chapters.synopsis: Fa·ro surveys the Colony from the central dais, disturbed that he doesn't look down on the Council Leaders despite being a god. He poses imposingly for watching Drones and contemplates having Sa·ma build a statue of him to ensure he's always remembered.

chapters.title: The World Builder
chapters.synopsis: Frankie startles Min while she's scrutinizing numbers. They debate whether the world is mathematical formulas or beautiful random patterns. Frankie invites Min to go collect textures, wanting to show her the real beauty in nature's patterns beyond equations.

chapters.title: Streets of Gold
chapters.synopsis: Sa·ma returns from the wilderness and shares his discovery of a mysterious cave with glowing plants and golden crystalline formations beneath the ground. The young Drones Ti·ni and Po·ni listen with fascination as he describes entering the unusual cave, unlike any he'd experienced before.

chapters.title: The Deleted File
chapters.synopsis: Olivia Holland's work journal entries document her first weeks as an intern: arriving on her first day, being dumped by boyfriend Randy via email, getting over the breakup with roommate Ashley's help, and settling into work analyzing data for Max.

chapters.title: God Like You
chapters.synopsis: Fa·ro forces Sa·ma to lead him, Ga·zo, and Za·zo back to the golden cave. Fa·ro believes it's his destiny to join the other gods through the portal to the 'Qubessence.' Sa·ma worries because the cave god told him to 'come back when it's done' and not to bring others. The portal appears to be closed.

chapters.title: The Exchange
chapters.synopsis: Ashley is home alone during a thunderstorm watching The Walking Dead. She hears mysterious knocking sounds that don't match the TV show, suggesting something unusual or supernatural is occurring in her apartment.

chapters.title: Confinement
chapters.synopsis: Sa·ma is imprisoned in the repurposed hollow interior of the Council building, the Polyans' first prison. Ga·zo visits to confirm he's alive per Fa·ro's wishes. They argue about the Colony's direction, with Ga·zo praising Fa·ro's godlike leadership while Sa·ma warns that nothing is unstoppable.

chapters.title: Coffee Break
chapters.synopsis: Min works late alone preparing for Graham's visit the next day. Her computer crashes with a blue screen system failure that won't respond to any inputs, threatening her critical calculations needed for the progress report.

chapters.title: Putsch
chapters.synopsis: The Council prepares to create a second seven-legged god as the violet bubble approaches the Source. Ga·zo convinced the Leaders this would provide backup for Fa·ro. Seven Leaders take positions near leg crystals around an empty seven-sided core crystal, with Ki·sa and Fa·ro watching to assist if needed.

chapters.title: The Shadow Room
chapters.synopsis: Min discovers a secret red-lit duplicate server room hidden beneath the Qube floor, with unauthorized cables and equipment. She finds a submarine-style hatch leading down to the lower floor where red light emanates.

chapters.title: Enslaved
chapters.synopsis: Fa·ro reveals he has eliminated the Council and declared himself sole ruler of the Colony. He drags Sa·ma before the assembled Polyans and announces the Council's death, blaming them for a failed attempt to create another god.

chapters.title: Split Decision
chapters.synopsis: Min accesses the secret server room computer using Olivia's password (6EQUJ5). She discovers Tor network software and Gridway distributed computing being used, realizing someone is conducting unauthorized operations, possibly weapons-related.

chapters.title: The Plan
chapters.synopsis: Hi·ma and Pu·ma travel to a secret meeting location with twelve lead Workers. Through flashback, Sa·ma is shown working on a mysterious contraption and having tense interactions with Fa·ro about the tower's construction.

chapters.title: Mala Sanctis
chapters.synopsis: Max confronts Min in the secret server room. She kicks the cabinet door into his face and tries to escape, but he trips her. They fight among spilled Qubes as Max pursues her through the damaged room.

chapters.title: The Machine
chapters.synopsis: Ga·zo reports to Fa·ro that all the Workers have vanished overnight. Using his artifact, Fa·ro scans and locates the Workers gathered near the tower in the distance beyond the Colony.

chapters.title: Button Mashup
chapters.synopsis: Min escapes to Dana's office and frantically reveals Max's secret room and weapon project. Dana locks the door and questions Min about what she discovered. Min explains her theory about Olivia's murder being connected to the secret project.

chapters.title: Storm Wind
chapters.synopsis: The Soldiers pursue the Workers but encounter a mysterious blue rock wall that blocks their path. Ga·zo orders them to find the edges of this barrier, which forms a long perpendicular clearing through the foliage.

chapters.title: The Incinerator
chapters.synopsis: Max, Min, and Dana confront each other in confusion. Max claims he's never been in the red room before and accuses Min. They hear sirens approaching. Max grabs both women and forces them through the Qube incinerator door, trapping them inside as it activates.

chapters.title: The Great Divide
chapters.synopsis: Hi·ma witnesses Sa·ma's device fire a beam that destroys two tower support columns. The massive tower falls across the Rift, creating a bridge to the other side. Workers begin crossing single-file to escape the approaching Soldiers.

chapters.title: Ashes to Ashes
chapters.synopsis: Trapped in the incinerator with depleting oxygen, Min and Max discover a corpse on a gurney. Max suggests using it as a ladder to reach the exhaust vent. Min climbs through and slides down into the chimney, searching for the clean-out door.

chapters.title: Into Battle
chapters.synopsis: The Soldiers massacre Workers at the bridge. Le·ma volunteers to be shot from Sa·ma's cannon. She's loaded into the barrel and launched through the air, crashing into Fa·ro and stealing his artifact before the Zalisk herd arrives to attack the Soldiers.

chapters.title: Clean-out
chapters.synopsis: Walter, a hospital candy striper, hears mysterious banging while transporting laundry in the basement. He follows the sound through storage cages to the incinerator chimney's clean-out door and decides to investigate by opening it.

chapters.title: Liberation
chapters.synopsis: The Zalisk herd, controlled by Le·ma using Fa·ro's stolen artifact, attacks only the Soldiers while ignoring the Workers. This allows the Workers to escape across the bridge. Fa·ro fights a Zalisk that pins him down as Ga·zo tries to help.

chapters.title: Projection
chapters.synopsis: Dana presents the quarterly report to Graham and the team, with Max and Min absent. Graham serves Dana coffee and expresses concern about Max's absence, emphasizing the importance of visionary leadership for the project's future.

chapters.title: Interruptions
chapters.synopsis: Instead of Dana's presentation, a video plays showing Dana confessing to Olivia's murder and her deal with DARPA for military funding in exchange for Costa Rican land. The video shows her locking Max in the incinerator. Captain Brennan enters to arrest her, but Dana draws a gun.

chapters.title: Hand of God
chapters.synopsis: Max commands Abina to pause the Spheria playback showing Polyans in a prayer circle. The team reviews recordings of the tower falling to create the bridge and the battle where Workers were slaughtered before the Zalisk intervention. The final revelation of the novel's climax begins.

novella1.title: Spheria: Olivia's Log
synopsis: Spheria: Olivia's Log is a prequel short story set entirely on Earth, following Olivia Holland, an intern on the Spheria Project at UConn Health Center in Farmington, Connecticut. The story opens with Olivia meditating on the roof of the Academic Building, her secret sanctuary, while processing a recent fight with her long-distance boyfriend Randy, who used a borrowed thermal imager to spy on her roommate Ashley in the shower.

Randy breaks up with Olivia by email shortly after, citing her as 'uptight,' which she processes with Ashley over drinks at the Butterfly lounge. When asked to make a sign for the lab's open house, Olivia retrieves poster boards from a storage room on the 6th floor and discovers something unusual: Dana, the Spheria Project Director, using a hidden keypad to access a secret room.

Using Randy's abandoned thermal imager, Olivia reads the residual heat on the keys and deduces the code — 2465. That night she returns alone, enters the room, and finds a clandestine second server room filled entirely with red Qubes — hardware that should have been incinerated — connected to an external network and running unsolved equations, apparently as part of a shadow project funded by an outside benefactor.

Olivia creates a hidden administrator account (passcode: 6EQUJ5, the Wow! Signal) to preserve access, but is caught by Dana returning to the room. Dana coerces Olivia into silence with a bribe of $400,000, threatening to name her as an accomplice if she talks. Olivia plays along while inwardly resolving to tell Max in the morning.

She encodes the floor number and keypad code into her journal using first letters of sentences, and emails it to Ashley as a safeguard — but the email fails to send due to a lost network connection, unbeknownst to her. On the roof that night, still wrestling with her decision, she is approached from behind by Dana and shoved to her death.

chapters.title: Meditation
chapters.synopsis: Olivia meditates on the roof of the Academic Building, interrupted by a seagull. She recalls Randy's recent visit — his use of a thermal imager to spy on her roommate Ashley led to a fierce argument and she kicked him out. The chapter establishes Olivia's character, her relationship with Ashley, and her sanctuary on the roof.

chapters.title: Breakup
chapters.synopsis: Randy sends a cold breakup email and asks for his thermal imager back. Olivia is devastated. Ashley takes her out to the Butterfly lounge for scorpion bowls and karaoke to lift her spirits. Olivia keeps Randy's device.

chapters.title: Open House
chapters.synopsis: Max asks Olivia to make a sign for the lab's open house. She goes to the 6th floor storage room and witnesses Dana entering a hidden room via a keypad in the dark. Using Randy's thermal imager, Olivia reads the residual heat signature on the keypad and deduces the code 2465. She takes the poster boards and leaves, planning to investigate further.

chapters.title: Keypad
chapters.synopsis: At 11pm, Olivia returns to the 6th floor storage room alone. She enters the hidden room and finds a secret server farm running entirely on red Qubes that should have been incinerated, connected to an outside network. She finds documents suggesting an illegal shadow project with money transfers. She creates a hidden admin account (passcode: 6EQUJ5 — the Wow! Signal) and is caught by Dana returning.

chapters.title: The Offer
chapters.synopsis: Dana confronts Olivia in the secret room. She reveals she has been diverting red Qubes and using them to solve complex problems for an outside benefactor, also manipulating Polyans inside Spheria to change their moral alignment. She offers Olivia $400,000 to stay silent and threatens to name her as an accomplice if she refuses. Olivia negotiates up from $250K and agrees under duress. Alone afterward, she encodes the keypad code and floor number into a journal entry using an acrostic cipher and tries to email it to Ashley as insurance — but the email fails to send.

chapters.title: Meditation
chapters.synopsis: Olivia returns to her rooftop sanctuary, weighing the offer against her conscience. She resolves to tell Max everything in the morning. The seagull reappears. As she stands at the edge looking down at a couple on the street below, Dana appears behind her and shoves her off the roof. Olivia's last word is 'No.'

book2.title: Spheria 2: Annulus
book2.subtitle: Annulus
synopsis: When a radical group of environmentalists decides the era of humans should end, they release an agent of mass extinction into the population. As the race for a cure begins around the world, the Spheria team volunteers the raw computing power of their project.

Can quantum computing find a solution faster than every other traditional research technique? Meanwhile, as the Polyans go about their lives, what impact will they have in all of this, unaware of the role they play?
